items.caption:
A poster from the national waste & recycling association, designed to educate the public on how to protect their trash and recycling workers from covid-19. the poster is divided into two main sections. the top section features a blue background with a white wave-like design and includes a warning about the airborne hazard of covid-19. it also includes a call to action to protect workers.

the bottom section of the poster is white with a blue border and contains a series of illustrations and text instructions. the illustrations depict a person in an orange shirt and blue jeans, a person in a red shirt and blue jeans, and a person in a red shirt and blue jeans with a green trash can. each person is shown interacting with a trash can in a different way, such as placing a bag in the can, lifting the lid, or putting a bag in the can.

the text instructions are organized into a checklist format with bullet points, advising the public to maintain social distance, keep recyclables loose, put all bags and recyclables into carts, keep lids on carts, and place only program recyclables in the recycling cart. there are also instructions to wipe down carts, especially the handles and lids, and to place any medical sharps or syringes in a recycling bin.

the poster is informative and uses a combination of visual elements and text to convey its message. the use of bright colors and clear illustrations makes the information easy to understand. the poster is likely intended for public display in areas where trash and recycling are collected, such as schools, offices, and public buildings.
